body.title: Day-close path-moves monitor — Python, file-index-backed, tickets/alarms non-canonical writes
body.description:
**Problem:** A renamed store with a surviving write-path silently split the source of truth (the misfiled decisions). Nothing detects an artifact written to a retired/non-canonical path. This is the regression guard so it can't happen invisibly again.

**Shape (Python only):** A path-moves registry (JSON) in the canonical store recording retired->canonical path mappings + the canonical-home rule per artifact type. A Python day-close check runs the registry against the file index (we already have a file/code index — reuse it, do not re-walk) and flags any dev-process artifact (decision/ticket/slate/etc.) found at a retired path or outside devlab/runtime/memory/. On a match it tickets/alarms (system_alarm or a filed ticket); a clean tree is silent. Runs inside day-close-audit.

**Affected files:** a new Python checker (e.g. devlab/claudecode/path_moves_monitor.py); a path_moves registry JSON under devlab/runtime/memory/rules/; day-close-audit skill wiring (one step).
**Design rules:** canonical-memory-location law; fail-soft (a monitor error never breaks day-close); read-only (never moves/deletes — surfaces only).
**Scope boundary:** IN — the registry + the Python monitor + day-close wiring + ticket/alarm on match. OUT — auto-remediation (surface only); the actual sweep (other tickets).
**Test plan:** plant a decision .json at a retired path (e.g. lab/design_docs/) in a temp tree -> monitor emits a finding/ticket naming the path; a clean canonical tree -> monitor silent; a monitor exception -> logged, day-close continues (fail-soft).
**Completion criteria:** the registry exists; the Python monitor flags a planted non-canonical artifact (ticket or alarm naming the offending path) and is silent on a clean tree; it runs in day-close-audit and never raises into it; it uses the existing file index rather than re-walking the repo.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Python day-close path-moves monitor: registry (devlab/runtime/memory/rules/path_moves.json) + monitor (devlab/claudecode/path_moves_monitor.py) scans the git file index, raises one deduped system alarm per retired root / per stray artifact; read-only, fail-soft; wired into day-close-audit Step 18.8. Proof: tests/test_path_moves_monitor.py::test_scan_flags_misfiled_artifacts_and_is_silent_on_clean_tree (red→green, 07f8ef55). Live tree: 2 grouped alarms, zero false positives.
